data.title: Vistagen (Nasdaq: VTGN) reports Q1 financial results for fiscal year 2026, ended June 30, 2025, in neuroscience development.
data.description: In South San Francisco, Vistagen (Nasdaq: VTGN), a biopharmaceutical company focused on neuroscience and developing intranasal pherines, released its fiscal year 2026 first quarter financial results ending June 30, 2025. The company highlighted progress in various pipeline programs. Shawn Singh, the President, expressed satisfaction with the quarter's productivity.

data.title: Western Forest Products Inc. reported Adjusted EBITDA of $0.5 million in Q2 2025 due to $3.6 million inventory provision expense.
data.description: Western Forest Products Inc. disclosed its financial results for the second quarter of 2025, posting an Adjusted EBITDA of $0.5 million. This figure reflects a significant inventory provision expense of $3.6 million attributed to the plummeting prices of specific lumber products. Contrasting this, the company recorded an Adjusted EBITDA of $9.4 million in the second quarter of 2024, inclusive of a $5.3 million recovery from inventory provisions. In the first quarter of 2025, Western reported an Adjusted EBITDA of $3.5 million, which encompassed an inventory provision expense of $1.8 million. The company's performance in the second quarter of 2025 was notably impacted by these financial factors.

data.title: Urgently Inc. (Nasdaq: ULY) to release second quarter 2025 financial results; date announced.
data.description: Urgent.ly Inc., a prominent digital roadside and mobility assistance technology provider based in the U.S., revealed that it will be releasing its financial results for the second quarter of 2025. The announcement was made on August 7, 2025, with the company trading under the Nasdaq symbol ULY. Urgent.ly is known for its innovative solutions in this sector and is anticipated to provide insights into its performance during this period. Investors and analysts are keenly awaiting the disclosed numbers to gauge the company's financial standing.

data.title: Stone Ridge buys Oklahoma assets from ConocoPhillips for $1.3 billion, 2nd acquisition this year.
data.description: Stone Ridge Holdings Group, a financial services company specializing in alternative asset management, reinsurance, and bitcoin, revealed today that its energy arm, Stone Ridge Energy, has finalized a deal to purchase a significant collection of Oklahoma energy assets from ConocoPhillips for around $1.3 billion. This acquisition follows SRE’s prior purchase of over $1 billion in energy assets in Colorado earlier this year, marking their second acquisition in 2023.

data.title: The Trade Desk (TTD) Q2 earnings report: $0.41/share earnings, $694.03M revenue, surpassing Street estimate. Stock plunges.
data.description: Trade Desk, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTD) announced its Q2 results after the market closed on Thursday. The company reported earnings of 41 cents per share, meeting analysts' expectations. Quarterly revenue stood at $694.03 million, surpassing the projected $684.99 million. For real-time TTD stock price and chart, please visit the website. Explore further details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: XPLR Infrastructure, LP Q2 2025 financial results released on company website. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: XPLR Infrastructure, LP, a company traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol XIFR, recently released its financial results for the second quarter of 2025. Interested parties can find the news release and accompanying presentation on the company's website at www.XPLRInfrastructure.com/FinancialResults. For further details, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: USPS reports controllable loss increased by $522 million in Q3 FY2025, with total net loss of $3.1 billion. Total revenue flat at $18.8 billion. New Postmaster General emphasizes financial strength and evolution for future sustainability. Operating expenses up by $613 million. Steiner reiterates need to enhance performance and address financial losses.
data.description: The U.S. Postal Service reported its financial results for Q3 of fiscal year 2025, with a controllable loss of $1.6 billion, up from $1.1 billion the previous year. The net loss for the quarter was $3.1 billion, compared to $2.5 billion in the same quarter last year. The increased losses were due to various factors including workers' compensation adjustments of $237 million, higher compensation and benefits expenses of $360 million, and increased other operating expenses of $205 million. Total operating revenue was $18.8 billion, with Shipping and Packages revenue up by $58 million, Marketing Mail revenue down by $29 million, and First-Class Mail revenue down by $86 million. Total operating expenses were $22.0 billion, up $613 million from the previous year. Postmaster General David Steiner emphasized the need for financial strength to meet the nation's needs and highlighted the importance of evolving to provide high-quality service at a reasonable cost.

data.title: Chesapeake Utilities Corporation's Q2 2025 results: Net income $23.9M, EPS $1.02, up 24.4% from Q2 2024. Revenue growth of $16.2M. Guidance: Adjusted EPS $6.15-$6.35 for 2025, capital expenditures $375M-$425M. Expansion projects in natural gas drive growth. Chair Jeff Householder optimistic for future growth.
data.description: Chesapeake Utilities Corporation reported strong financial results for the second quarter of 2025, with a net income of $23.9 million and EPS of $1.02, while for the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income was $74.8 million and EPS was $3.22. Adjusted net income and EPS, excluding specific expenses, were $24.3 million and $1.04 respectively for the quarter, and $75.4 million and $3.25 for the six months. Adjusted gross margin grew by $16.2 million and $34.1 million for the respective periods due to various initiatives. They issued a $200 million long-term debt agreement in August 2025 and maintained their 2025 Adjusted EPS guidance of $6.15 - $6.35. The capital guidance for 2025 was raised to $375 - $425 million due to project advances. The Company reaffirmed its 2028 EPS guidance of $7.75 to $8.00 and capital expenditure guidance of $1.5 billion to $1.8 billion for 2024-2028. Operating income for the second quarter increased by $9.5 million compared to 2024, with the Regulated Energy Segment contributing significantly to the growth. Adjusted gross margin increase was driven by various factors, including natural gas demand, infrastructure projects, and customer consumption. Overall, the Company showcased strong financial performance and growth strategies in the reported period.

data.title: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. announced Q2 2025 results: Same store sales increased 2.1%, Royalty Pool sales up 3.9%. EPS increased by 1.3%. Royalty Pool System Sales reached $161.4 million for the quarter, $312.7 million for the period. Dividends were $5.7 million for the quarter, $11.4 million for the period. 794 restaurants in Royalty Pool in 2025. Credit facility extended with 1.19:1 D/E ratio and 29.47:1 interest coverage ratio.
data.description: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. released financial results showing an increase in Same Store Sales (SSSG) and Royalty Pool Sales for both the second quarter and year-to-date periods ending June 30, 2025. In the second quarter, Royalty Pool system sales rose 3.9% to $161.4 million, with Pizza Pizza sales up 4.1% and Pizza 73 sales up 2.4%. Year-to-date, system sales increased 2.8% to $312.7 million. SSSG increased 2.1% for the quarter and 1.6% for the period. Adjusted earnings per share rose 1.3% for the quarter to $0.241. The Company's dividends remained unchanged at $0.2325 per share for the quarter and $0.4650 per share for the period, with a payout ratio of 108% for the quarter and 112% for the period. The Company increased its restaurant network by 3 net locations and opened new traditional and non-traditional Pizza Pizza and Pizza 73 restaurants. The credit facility was extended, and the Company maintained financial ratios within covenants. Overall, the Company reported positive growth and financial stability in the face of industry challenges.

data.title: Saltire Capital Ltd. reports Q2 2025 financial results with revenue of $9.3M, 23.8% higher YoY. Net loss at $11.4M. Completed acquisition of SanStone Investments.(CharSequence: 257)
data.description: Saltire Capital Ltd. reported unaudited financial results for the three and six-month period ending June 30, 2025. The company's revenue for the six months ending June 30, 2025, was $9.3 million, a 23.8% increase from the same period in 2024. Gross profit increased to $3.8 million with gross margins of 40.9%. Operating income was $1.2 million, while the net loss was $11.4 million due to a non-cash fair value loss of $11.3 million on warrant liabilities. Adjusted EBITDA for the period was $1.0 million. Saltire acquired SanStone Investments Limited, a heavy equipment and agricultural dealership operator in Eastern Canada, with a loan agreement securing up to $100 million credit facilities for future growth. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Guild Holdings Company (NYSE: GHLD) reports strong Q2 2025 results, with highest performance in years despite market challenges.
data.description: In San Diego, Guild Holdings Company (NYSE: GHLD) shared its second-quarter results for the period ending June 30, 2025. The mortgage company, known for its relationship-focused loan approach, highlighted significant achievements. The quarter showed exceptional performance compared to previous years, despite market limitations and difficulties. Guild's team demonstrated strength across various categories, marking this period as one of their best in recent times.

data.title: HEI reports Q2 2025 net income of $26M ($0.15/share), Core1 income $35M ($0.20/share), up from $28M ($0.26/share) in 2024.
data.description: In a recent report, Hawaiian Electric Industries, Inc. (HEI) announced a net income of $26 million for the second quarter of 2025, translating to $0.15 per share. When factoring out costs related to the Maui wildfire and expenses associated with assessing strategic options for Pacific Current, the company achieved a Core1 income from continuing operations of $35 million or $0.20 per share. This marks an improvement from the previous year's figures of $28 million or $0.26 per share. The company expressed satisfaction with the performance of its core operations during this period.

data.title: i3 Verticals reports Q3 2025 revenue of $51.9M, up 12.4% YoY, with 9 months revenue at $158M.
data.description: i3 Verticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: IIIV) announced its financial results for the fiscal third quarter ending June 30, 2025. Revenue from continuing operations totaled $51.9 million in the third quarter, marking a 12.4% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the nine months ending June 30, 2025, revenue from continuing operations stood at $158 million.

data.title: NuScale Power's Q2 2025 results announced, ended June 30th.
data.description: NuScale Power Corporation has reported their financial results for the second quarter ending on June 30, 2025. The company trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol SMR.

data.title: HASI reported Q2 2025 results with GAAP EPS of $0.74 (up from $0.23 in Q2 2024) & Adjusted EPS of $0.60 (down from $0.63 in Q2 2024).
data.description: Annapolis, Maryland - Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc. (NYSE: HASI) released its second-quarter 2025 results. The company reported a GAAP EPS of $0.74, up from $0.23 in Q2 2024, and an Adjusted EPS of $0.60, down from $0.63 in Q2 2024. GAAP-based Net Investment Income (Loss) for the quarter stood at $(3) million, excluding Income from Equity Method Investments.HASI is a prominent investor in sustainable infrastructure assets.

data.title: Amprius Technologies reports Q2 2025 results for Silicon Anode Platform with high-performance batteries.
data.description: Amprius Technologies, Inc. based in Fremont, California, and listed as AMPX on the NYSE, revealed its Q2 2025 financial performance. The company, known for its Silicon Anode Platform in lithium-ion batteries, shared the update via a shareholder letter on ir.amprius.com. The report includes information on the delivery of advanced batteries and other business endeavors. For more details, interested individuals can access the investor relations website.

data.title: Energy Vault (NYSE: NRGV) released Q2 2025 financial results, highlighting growth in key markets and progress in Australia and U.S. expansions.
data.description: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NRGV), a prominent player in sustainable grid-scale energy storage sector, recently disclosed its financial performance for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company showcased significant advancements in key growth regions by successfully implementing core strategies. Notably, progress was made on constructing two energy storage projects in Australia, alongside unveiling plans for expanding operations within the U.S. These developments highlight the company's commitment to innovation and market expansion.

data.title: Stem Inc. reports $38.4M revenue in Q2 2025, up 13% from previous year. GAAP gross profit $12.8M.
data.description: In Houston, Stem, Inc. (NYSE: STEM), a prominent player in AI-powered clean energy solutions, shared its financial performance for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company reported revenue of $38.4 million, marking a 13% increase from $34.0 million in the same period last year. GAAP gross profit stood at $12.8 million, up from $9.4 million in the previous year's second quarter. Furthermore, non-GAAP gross profit rose to $18.7 million compared to $13.5 million in 2Q24. The GAAP gross margin also saw a positive trajectory, reaching 33% in this quarter.

data.title: ESS (NYSE: GWH) to hold conference call on Aug 14, 2025, to discuss Q2 financial results ending June 30.
data.description: Wilsonville, Oregon-based ESS Tech, Inc. (NYSE: GWH), a key player in iron flow long-duration energy storage systems for commercial and utility-scale usage, has disclosed plans for a conference call scheduled on Thursday, August 14, 2025, at 5:00 p.m. EDT. The purpose of the call is to delve into the financial outcomes of the second quarter of 2025, concluding on June 30, 2025. The official announcement regarding the financial standing and results from the mentioned quarter is set to be released on the same day as the conference call.
